title: coris monspeliensis subsp. syrtica
intro: Coris monspeliensis subsp. syrtica, also known as Sardinian bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Coris monspeliensis subsp. syrtica include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Coris monspeliensis subsp. syrtica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments in traditional medicine.
